name: Greenworks 40V Brushless Cordless Leaf Blower with 2Ah Battery
whatWorks: Battery and charger are included in the kit — at $179 CAD, this represents genuine value compared to EGO and DEWALT listings where the battery must be purchased separately.

150 CFM is solidly in the mid-range — enough to clear a typical Canadian residential driveway, patio, and deck in one session, and handles moderate autumn leaf volumes without multiple passes on flat surfaces.
Brushless motor runs cooler, more efficiently, and with a longer service life than brushed alternatives at the same price point. The longevity advantage compounds over years of seasonal use.
Compatible with Greenworks 40V batteries from the mower lineup — buyers who already purchased the Greenworks 40V 17" mower get immediate cross-tool battery sharing.
worthKnowing: The Greenworks 40V ecosystem has fewer tool options than DEWALT 20V or RYOBI 40V — limited hedger, chainsaw, and snow blower options compared to those platforms. Expansion is possible but more constrained.
150 CFM is significantly less than EGO's 650 CFM — for heavy autumn leaf cleanup on larger Canadian properties, multiple sessions or passes will be needed.
Runtime of 20–40 minutes varies widely by speed setting. Turbo mode on heavy debris approaches the lower end; light-speed cleanup on dry surfaces reaches the upper end.
Greenworks customer support has slower response times than premium brands — Canadian buyers should be aware warranty resolution can take several weeks.

commonComplaints.title: 40V ecosystem has fewer tool options…
commonComplaints.body: 40V ecosystem has fewer tool options than RYOBI or EGO. I wanted to add a hedge trimmer and the selection was limited.

commonComplaints.title: Not as powerful as EGO for…
commonComplaints.body: Not as powerful as EGO for heavy wet autumn leaves. Gets them moving but takes more passes on my larger yard.

commonComplaints.title: Customer service took three weeks to…
commonComplaints.body: Customer service took three weeks to respond to my warranty question. Product works fine but support is slow.

commonComplaints.title: Turbo mode drains the 2Ah battery…
commonComplaints.body: Turbo mode drains the 2Ah battery in about 20 minutes. Works well but I'd recommend upgrading to a 4Ah battery if you have a bigger yard.

verdict: The Greenworks 40V Brushless blower sits at a practical midpoint: more airflow than DEWALT's 100 CFM entry-level, battery included unlike most EGO listings, and brushless for longevity. The 40V ecosystem is the main limitation for long-term expansion. For buyers who want a complete kit at $179 CAD and have a medium-sized Canadian property, it's a solid choice. Heavy-duty autumn leaf management on larger lots will want EGO's 650 CFM.

realBuyers.quote: Battery and charger included at $179 — I was ready to go out of the box. That alone makes it worth recommending.
Handles fall leaves on my suburban lot no problem. Took one full session to clear everything.
Quieter than my old gas blower — I can finally blow off the driveway without waking everyone at 8am.
Lightweight and easy to use for an extended session. Arm doesn't get tired the way it did with heavier models.

sources.title: Best Budget Cordless Leaf Blowers — Bob Vila
Greenworks Outdoor Tools Review — This Old House

metaDescription: Greenworks 40V brushless cordless leaf blower review for Canada: 150 CFM, battery included, 40V ecosystem. Honest on ecosystem limits and heavy-leaf performance. ~$179 CAD.
introText: The Greenworks 40V Brushless Cordless Leaf Blower delivers 150 CFM with battery and charger included — making it a complete mid-range package for residential yard cleanup. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07YNKC7RW) at around $179 CAD.
